vehicles.slot: Cap or slot near gear selector base on console; 11th gen (2023+): slot near electronic selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap or slot near gear selector base on console; 11th gen (2023+): slot near electronic selector.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Manual trans: no shift lock — depress clutch.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.warnings: Hybrid: 12V auxiliary under hood only — never touch IMA/HV battery.

vehicles.trans: 4-speed or 5-speed auto, or CVT (varies by generation)
vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near gear selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.notes: AWD optional.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.trans: 4-speed or 5-speed auto, CVT, or DCT (varies by generation)
vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DCT dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Cap near gear selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DCT transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.notes: Type R: manual only — clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near gear selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near gear selector base on console.
vehicles.notes: AWD optional.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Slot on right side or front of selector housing — use PHYSICAL KEY from key fob as the tool
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Slot on right side or front of selector housing.
OVERRIDE TOOL: Pop out the PHYSICAL KEY from inside the key fob (press the release button on the fob to eject it) — this is the override tool for this vehicle.
Insert the physical key into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
The physical key inside the key fob is required for the mechanical override — always carry a spare key.
vehicles.notes: 3rd gen Pilot key fob contains a hidden physical key — use this as the tool for the shift lock slot. ZF 9HP: jump pack preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: Physical Key from Fob Is the Override Tool
knownIssues.detail: The 3rd gen Pilot (2016-2022) shift lock slot requires a thin key to actuate. The override tool is the PHYSICAL KEY hidden inside the key fob — pop it out using the release button on the fob. Operators without this key or who don't know about it cannot complete the override without improvising a thin tool.

battery.warnings: Key fob physical key is the shift lock tool on this generation.

vehicles.slot: Slot near electronic gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Slot near electronic gear selector base on console.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console; special ZF 9HP override tool also available
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
ZF 9HP transmission: locate the shift lock slot — Cap near gear selector base on console; special ZF 9HP override tool also available.
Insert a thin key or dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 from dealer tool suppliers) into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
NOTE: The ZF 9HP solenoid requires more force than most vehicles — apply steady firm pressure.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
ZF 9HP STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
The ZF 9HP is very difficult to shift without 12V power — jumping the battery first is the most reliable method.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
If using mechanical slot: a dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is more reliable than a key.
vehicles.notes: ZF 9HP: use jump pack or special tool. Do not force shift without power.
vehicles.warn: Do not force shift without power — ZF 9HP is sensitive to improper manual shifting.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Requires Power or Special Tool
knownIssues.detail: The 5th gen Odyssey uses a ZF 9HP 9-speed transmission that requires 12V power to shift to neutral reliably. The manual override slot is difficult without power. A dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 from dealer tool suppliers) is the most reliable mechanical method.

knownIssues.title: Do Not Force the Shift Without Power
knownIssues.detail: Forcing the ZF 9HP transmission to neutral without power risks damaging the transmission's park pawl and internal components. Jump the battery first whenever possible.

battery.warnings: ZF 9HP: must restore power before neutral shift. Use jump pack — ZF 9HP override tool is the most reliable manual method.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near gear selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near gear selector base on console.
vehicles.notes: FWD minivan.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start).
Honda confirmed the Insight e-CVT CANNOT be shifted without 12V power on most units.
Locate the shift lock slot near the selector base — this may not work without power.
Strongly recommended: jump the 12V battery first before attempting any override.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
POWER REQUIRED: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move selector to N normally.
If power cannot be restored, use wheel dollies under the driven wheels — do NOT attempt to force.
vehicles.notes: Honda officially confirmed power is required. Slot method may not override on all units. Always jump pack first.
vehicles.warn: POWER REQUIRED on most units. Do not force — use dollies if no power is available.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

knownIssues.title: Cannot Shift to Neutral Without 12V Power
knownIssues.detail: Honda officially confirmed that the 3rd gen Insight e-CVT hybrid CANNOT be shifted to neutral without battery power on most units. The manual slot override does not function without power on this model. ALWAYS jump the 12V battery first. If no power source is available, use vehicle dollies rather than attempting to force the drivetrain.

battery.warnings: POWER REQUIRED to shift to neutral on most units. Jump first — do not attempt without power.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near gear selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near gear selector base on console.
vehicles.notes: AWD standard on all Ridgeline.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

knownIssues.title: AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: All Ridgeline trims have full-time AWD (i-VTM4) that cannot be disabled. There is no 2WD mode or transfer case position. FLATBED ONLY on all Ridgelines. Do not attempt wheel-lift towing on any Ridgeline.

battery.warnings: AWD standard — flatbed only on all Ridgelines.

vehicles.slot: Small cap or slot at selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap at selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual trans: depress clutch, Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console — behind the selector, not at base front
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the small cap BEHIND (toward rear) the gear selector — not at the front base.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly downward.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch, move to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Slot is BEHIND the selector on 6th gen — same principle as later Camry. Manual: depress clutch.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console

AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual trans: depress clutch, Neutral. Very common vehicle.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.

vehicles.notes: Real Time AWD: flatbed preferred. FWD: lift from front.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (key to Run position — do not start).
Locate the shift lock override: small cap near selector base on center console.
Pry off the plastic cap with your ignition key or a small flathead screwdriver.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral. ATTS system on some — FWD only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

vehicles.slot: Manual transmission only — no shift lock slot
shift: Manual transmission only — no automatic shift lock.
Depress the clutch pedal fully.
Move the gear lever to Neutral.
Do not attempt to use a shift lock slot — there is none.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Depress the clutch and select Neutral.
If clutch cannot be depressed (power issue), manually move the gear lever — it will slide to Neutral without clutch pressure when stationary.
Battery is under the hood if a jump is needed.
vehicles.notes: Manual transmission ONLY — no automatic, no shift lock slot. High-value sports car.
vehicles.warn: High-value vehicle — flatbed preferred. Manual only — no shift lock slot.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed strongly preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
High-value vehicle — use flatbed with padded wheel grids whenever possible.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: AWD uses Honda Real Time 4WD (on-demand rear coupling) — no traditional TC. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
AWD (Real Time 4WD): no traditional TC — flatbed preferred.

neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.

vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. CVT: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: CVT on most trims: flatbed preferred. Manual (2016-2021 FWD): depress clutch, select Neutral. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: CVT: flatbed preferred. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. CVT/AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground.
AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: AWD uses Honda i-VTM4 torque-vectoring AWD — no traditional TC. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred. AWD without flatbed: dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
AWD (i-VTM4): no traditional TC — flatbed preferred.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — no traditional shift lock slot
shift: Turn ignition/power to ON.
PHEV/EV: press the N button on the electronic gear selector.
Hold N button for 2 seconds while pressing the brake pedal.
Verify N is shown on the instrument display.
If power is lost: restore 12V auxiliary power first.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to 12V auxiliary battery — under the hood, driver side.
PHEV/EV: 12V auxiliary battery ONLY — NEVER touch the high-voltage battery.
Once 12V is restored, press N button to select Neutral.
PHEV: lift from front (FWD). EV and FCV: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.notes: Three variants: PHEV (most common), EV, and FCV (fuel cell). All are FWD. High-voltage battery under floor. 12V auxiliary under hood.
vehicles.warn: PHEV/EV/FCV: NEVER touch HV battery. 12V auxiliary ONLY for jumping.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front for PHEV. EV/FCV: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: PHEV: FWD, lift from front.
EV and FCV variants: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain.
NEVER access high-voltage battery. 12V auxiliary is under the hood.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. 12V auxiliary only.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to 12V battery — under the hood.
12V battery ONLY — never touch the IMA hybrid battery under the rear.
Once powered, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.notes: IMA mild hybrid. 12V under hood. IMA battery under rear cargo area. Manual: depress clutch. CVT: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: CVT: flatbed preferred. HYBRID: 12V under hood ONLY — never touch IMA battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. CVT: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
CVT: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground.
HYBRID: 12V under hood only — never touch IMA battery.

battery.warnings: IMA hybrid: 12V under hood ONLY. Never touch IMA battery under cargo area.
